facts.text: The Lenni‑Lenape people once seasonally visited Lavallette’s barrier island beaches to harvest fish and shellfish, though no permanent settlements existed.
Lavallette’s transformation began in 1881 when a railroad reached the island, opening the area to inland visitors and boosting resort development.
In December 1887 the New Jersey Legislature incorporated Lavallette as a borough, naming it after Rear Admiral Elie A. to honor his naval legacy.
Today, the borough is developing an inclusive playground at Chandler Park, funded through the NJ Green Acres Program and local engineering partners.
